objections.claim: The proposed single `workflow.json` has only record-level `status` and `normativity` under the frozen schema, yet the approach assigns normativity per stage without defining separate schema-valid records. This cannot faithfully represent the required mixed posture. The approach must either make the workflow record ADVISORY while authority-linking the existing NORMATIVE-CURRENT invariant records, or split independently normative and advisory claims into separate records.

The human projection is conditional and permits “a note in the module README index.” The frozen schema requires machine-first, generated human projection and identifies the per-component README as rendered. A hand-maintained fallback would create an independent drift surface. The approach must specify a deterministic rendered projection from the machine record.

The promised structural check has no executable mechanism, file, or command. Running `verify-contracts.mjs` unchanged merely “to prove no disturbance” does not establish that the new stage references resolve or are content-addressed. The approach must name a concrete executable oracle for reference resolution and content-address validation, without turning the advisory documentation-stage ordering into shared enforcement.

The authority basis abbreviates the frozen full commit anchor to `git:6d16c8f`. A load-bearing authority statement must terminate in a stable identifier; the approach must preserve the full frozen anchor `git:6d16c8ff7cb120910ed0c2968d321269dd1f57cb` and the directive’s exact file reference.
